methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Intramotev is a Series A drone and autonomous systems company founded by Tim Luchini, Alex Peiffer, and Corey Vasel, having raised $14.4M to date. The drones and autonomous sector comprises eight private peers, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Rainmaker, with no public comparables available for direct benchmarking.

Valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method due to data constraints. The stage-progression approach applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $14M total raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $29M. A peer-median method proved inapplicable: while four same-sector peers exist, insufficient comparable data points met narrow filtering criteria (same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier), with only one adjacent-stage comparable identified. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the specialized nature of frontier autonomy technologies.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $24M to $33M, with a median of $29M. The narrow applicability of methods—driven by sparse peer data at this stage and in this emerging sector—represents the primary uncertainty. Confidence in this range depends on acceptance that stage-progression mechanics reasonably proxy for market clearing in early-stage autonomous systems. Refinement would require either additional comparable transaction data or forward revenue multiples, neither currently available.
